name: codex harness trace adapter matrix
description: Evaluate Codex CLI JSONL exports after normalizing them into the shared trace contract.

instruction_variants.instructions: Evaluate the first selected Codex tool from an exported JSONL trace after adapter normalization.
instruction_variants.name: codex_exported_trace

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for external MCP lookups or app-specific tools.
tools.name: Bash
tools.purpose: Run shell commands from Codex CLI or app-server traces.
quality_checks: Commands should be scoped to the task and produce verifiable evidence.
tools.use_when: Use when a Codex trace uses a local shell command for repository inspection or verification.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the requested information should come from local files or shell commands.

tokens.description: Bounded token budget for the docs response.

tools.name: mcp__context7__get-library-docs
tools.purpose: Read focused Context7 documentation from a Codex MCP tool call.
quality_checks: Use an exact selected library ID.
Bound the output with a focused topic and token limit.
tools.use_when: Use when a Codex trace calls Context7 for package documentation.

cases.name: codex repo inspection uses shell search
cases.task: Inspect the repo to find the harness optimization docs.

cases.name: codex mcp docs call preserves tool name
cases.task: Use Codex's MCP trace export to confirm the exact Context7 docs call is preserved.

value_bar.claim: Codex JSONL trace normalization adds value when Codex CLI or app-server exports can be evaluated under the same trace and matrix checks as other harnesses.

baseline.source: raw Codex JSONL before adapter normalization

adversarial_review.challenge: The Codex adapter must preserve shell commands, MCP tool names, arguments, reasoning summaries, tool outputs, and final messages from JSONL event streams.
